---
title: "Building a Model (Python)"
author: "Simon Frost"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
  gfm:
    toc: true
engine: python
---

## Component-based setup

```{python}
import starsim as ss

n_contacts = 10
beta = 0.5 / n_contacts
net = ss.RandomNet(n_contacts=n_contacts)
disease = ss.SIR(beta=beta, dur_inf=4, init_prev=0.02)

sim = ss.Sim(
    n_agents=1_000,
    networks=net,
    diseases=disease,
    dt=1.0,
    start=0,
    stop=40,
    rand_seed=42,
    verbose=0,
)
sim.run()
```

## Multiple networks

```{python}
n_contacts_hh = 4
n_contacts_work = 8
beta = 0.5 / (n_contacts_hh + n_contacts_work)

sim_multi = ss.Sim(
    n_agents=1_000,
    networks=[
        ss.RandomNet(name='household', n_contacts=n_contacts_hh),
        ss.RandomNet(name='workplace', n_contacts=n_contacts_work),
    ],
    diseases=ss.SIR(beta=beta, dur_inf=4, init_prev=0.01),
    dt=1.0, start=0, stop=40, rand_seed=42, verbose=0,
)
sim_multi.run()

prev = sim_multi.results.sir.prevalence.values
print(f"Peak prevalence with 2 networks: {max(prev):.4f}")
```

## Exporting to DataFrame

```{python}
df = sim.to_df()
print(f"Columns: {list(df.columns)[:10]}...")
df.head()
```
